items.role: Bayesian Estimation of RANK and HANK Business Cycle Models
Tools for Macroeconomists Summer School: The Essentials
European Association of Young Economists Annual Meeting

items.details: Winner of the Warwick Award for Teaching Excellence (Social Sciences Faculty Award), in recognition of an outstanding contribution to teaching and student support. The Social Sciences Faculty Award recognises the achievements of Warwick's outstanding educators who have enabled excellent learning, creating the conditions within which all students are supported and empowered to succeed and thrive

Awarded Fellowship of Advance HE following successful completion of the Professional Pathway for Experienced Staff (APP EXP) during the 2023–2024 academic year. The programme, accredited by Advance HE, supports experienced staff in demonstrating alignment with the UK Professional Standards Framework (UKPSF) 2023 and recognising their contribution to teaching and learning support in higher education.

Nominated, shortlisted as finalist, and awarded a commendation (3rd prize) in the Warwick Award for Teaching Excellence (WATE Social Sciences Faculty Award), in recognition of outstanding contributions to teaching and the support of student learning.

Nominated and shortlisted as a finalist in the Student Teaching Award for Best Graduate Teaching Assistant of the University of Glasgow. The award recognises teachers who have made an excellent contribution to the learning experience, inspired students to study beyond the classroom, and show clear passion and excitement for their subject matter.

Certificate of Excellence in Teaching for passing the Developing as a Teacher in Higher Education course (DAT HE) during the 2018-2019 academic year: The DAT HE course has been developed in alignment with Descriptor I of the UK’s Professional Standards Framework for Teaching and Supporting Learning (Associate Fellowship).

items.role: Topics in Financial Economics 3rd year Undergraduate (EC333-EC334)

items.details: Serve as an external dissertation supervisor for the University of Glasgow, providing academic guidance to 5–10 MSc students each year throughout their dissertation projects.

items.role: Project Supervisor in the Undergraduate Research Support Scheme
items.details: Serves as an academic supervisor for the Undergraduate Research Support Scheme (URSS), mentoring an average of one undergraduate student per year in the design and completion of an independent summer research project.

items.role: Warwick Summer School: Money and Banking
items.details: Designed and have been delivering a course on Money and Banking, comprising four three-hour lectures, at the Warwick Summer School.

items.role: MSc Finance & Economics: Economics Summer School
items.details: Designed and delivered asynchronous online learning materials in macroeconomics for non-economics students preparing to enter the MSc Finance & Economics, jointly offered by Warwick Business School and the Department of Economics.

items.role: Warwick Pre-University Summer School: An Introduction to the History of Economic Thoughts
items.details: Designed and delivered a mini-lecture for prospective economics students at the Warwick Pre-University Summer School.

items.role: Warwick Pre-University Summer School: An Introduction to Macroeconomics
items.details: Designed and delivered a mini-lecture for prospective economics students at the Warwick Pre-University Summer School.

items.role: Jury Member, Warwick Awards for Teaching Excellence (WATE)
items.details: Evaluate university staff nominations for teaching awards and contribute to the recognition of excellence in teaching and student support.

Led the review of undergraduate and postgraduate macroeconomics curricula. Conducted comprehensive curriculum mapping to identify gaps and overlaps across modules, and benchmarked programmes against competitor institutions to inform recommendations for curriculum development.
Invited panelist at the Warwick Education Forum to contribute to discussions on teaching excellence and the future of education strategy at Warwick.

items.role: Coordinator, MSc Macro and Monetary Economics Dissertation Group
items.details: Support MSc students in developing dissertation topics, refining research questions, and identifying appropriate supervisors, while providing ongoing academic and pastoral guidance throughout the dissertation process.
Support the professional development of Graduate Teaching Assistants through teaching observations, structured feedback, and reflective discussions on pedagogical practice.

Support communication and engagement between TF colleagues and departmental leadership, organise meetings, coordinate evidence-based initiatives and feedback mechanisms, and contribute to the improvement of departmental culture and operations.
Senior invigilator in main examination halls and invigilator for mitigating circumstances examinations.
Contribute to MSc and undergraduate recruitment and marketing activities through student feedback analysis, participation in Open Days, and attendance at graduation ceremonies.

items.role: Co-lead, Warwick Internship Scheme for Economists
items.details: Review and shortlist research internship proposals, support colleagues with recruitment processes, and participate in student interviews.

Personal Tutor, providing academic guidance, mentoring, and pastoral support to approximately 27–30 students each year. Academic Referee, writing 25–45 comprehensive reference letters annually for current students and alumni.
